macro_summary: Oil surged 10% to $109/bbl overnight as US-Iran talks collapsed and the White House planned an extended blockade of Iranian ports, per macro_news. This reignites inflation fears and hardens the Fed's hawkish stance ahead of today's BigTech earnings batch (AMZN, GOOG, MSFT). SPY edged -0.12%, QQQ +0.33%, as energy gains offset tech caution; VIX jumped 5.5% to 18.81. The oil shock is now the dominant macro driver, compressing equity risk appetite into the triple-earnings binary after the close.
commodities_note: WTI spiked $10.07 to $109.40 — the sharpest single-day gain since the Hormuz crisis began, validating the $100-135/bbl analyst range. Gold eased to $4,571. DXY rose 0.32% to 98.99 on haven flows. US10Y climbed 6bp to 4.42%, breaking above the 4.40% hawkish threshold. The energy premium is structural, not tactical — expect sustained cost pressure on logistics and semiconductors.

writeup.headline: Promoted to AVOID — China AV permit suspension per Bloomberg on April 29.
writeup.what_to_do: China suspended new autonomous vehicle licenses, directly blocking WRD's robotaxi expansion in its home market. The commercial-traction leg of the thesis is now on hold. Avoid until either (a) permits resume with clear timeline, or (b) price capitulates below $6.85 to fully discount the regulatory freeze.

singapore.summary: The Singapore cluster remains under structural NIM pressure with US10Y at 4.42%, now $0.22 above the 4.20% reset threshold. DBS reports tonight — that print will either reinforce or challenge the bear case. OCBC is the relative safe haven; UOB struggles below its 50-day average.
singapore.thesis_status: NIM compression ongoing, DBS earnings key.
singapore.key_development: DBS reports tonight — NIM trajectory will signal whether margin relief is approaching.

semiconductors.summary: SOXX recovered 1.4% but the complex is bifurcated: mega-caps (NVDA +12%, AMD +47%, INTC +77%) are deep in extreme extension, while foundry plays like ASML and MU are structurally stronger yet now face oil-driven logistics cost inflation. Tonight's BigTech capex commentary is the binary that can either justify rich valuations or trigger mean-reversion.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Bifurcation sustains; oil cost headwind new.
semiconductors.key_development: BigTech earnings tonight — capex guidance is the binary for semis revenue expectations.

big_tech.summary: The cluster enters a high-stakes binary with AMZN, GOOG, MSFT reporting. The capex moderation thesis will be stress tested; Google holds the cleanest technical position among them. Microsoft's OpenAI concentration risk adds an extra layer of scrutiny. PLTR remains in the valuation penalty box.
big_tech.key_development: Earnings tonight from AMZN, GOOG, MSFT — Azure AI revenue quality, AWS growth, and capex outlook are the key variables.

interconnected.name: Coherent Corp
interconnected.reason: NVDA's $2B photonics investment partner — tied to LITE's AI optics ramp; any NVDA demand signal lifts both.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if LITE earnings on May 6 confirm order uptake and COHR pulls back 8-10% from current levels.

interconnected.name: Exxon Mobil Corp
interconnected.reason: Direct beneficiary of the WTI spike to $109 and extended Hormuz blockade — clean energy-sector hedge against oil-driven inflation.
interconnected.entry_note: Worth watching if WTI holds above $100 through the May 12 CPI print and XOM pulls back 5-7% to its 50-day average.

interconnected.name: Samsung Electronics Co Ltd
interconnected.reason: Competing with TSMC and INTC on advanced logic; today's news that it expects more 2nm customers adds a foundry counterweight to the semis cluster.
interconnected.entry_note: Revisit after TSMC's next capacity update; add if Samsung pulls back to pre-news levels in a 10% correction.

interconnected.name: SK Hynix Inc
interconnected.reason: Major HBM supplier to NVDA — tight HBM capacity and surging capex validate the memory demand thesis that supports MU.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if MU earnings on June 25 confirm HBM pricing and SK Hynix dips 8-10% from its recent highs.

interconnected.name: Arista Networks Inc
interconnected.reason: AI datacenter networking pure-play — benefits from hyperscaler capex and the optical interconnect buildout tied to LITE/COHR.
interconnected.entry_note: Becomes interesting if tonight's BigTech capex commentary surprises to the upside and ANET retraces to its 50-day average.

active_risks: US-Iran Hormuz blockade escalation after peace talks collapsed — Trump cancels mission, White House plans extended port blockade per WSJ.
WTI surges to $109.40, threatening a structural energy-cost shock that reignites inflation and hardens the Fed's hawkish stance.
Middle East airspace closures and tanker reroutes compound logistics cost pressure on global supply chains, particularly semiconductors.
China suspends autonomous driving permits after Baidu outage — a direct regulatory blow to robotaxi operators like WRD.
geopolitical.market_impact: Oil above $100 has slammed the door on near-term Fed rate cuts, pushing US10Y to 4.42% and compressing BigTech valuations. Semis are caught between structural AI demand and oil-driven cost inflation — ASML and MU face margin pressure, while NVDA and AMD's extreme extensions look even more vulnerable. The Singapore banks' NIM compression deepens as US rates stay elevated.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: US-Iran peace talks collapsed over the weekend; Trump canceled the negotiating mission and the White House is now planning an extended blockade of Iranian ports per WSJ.
geopolitical.portfolio_action: Stay defensive — with WTI above $100 and BigTech earnings pending, no new entries until post-earnings clarity and VIX settles below 20.

events_this_week.event: BigTech triple earnings (AMZN, GOOG, MSFT) — capex guidance binary
AMD and LITE earnings after close — MI450/Meta GPU shipment clarity and NVDA photonics orders in focus
FOMC Rate Decision — likely hold, but dot-plot shift would move rate expectations
DBS earnings — NIM trajectory critical for Singapore bank cluster
PLTR earnings after close — defense AI and commercial deals will validate high valuation
JOLTS Report (March) — labor market tightness check before FOMC
UOB earnings — NIM update for the Singapore cluster
OCBC earnings — the Singapore bank with the best NIM resilience

portfolio_snapshot.overall_stance: Defensive — oil shock and BigTech earnings binary drive caution.
portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: Hormuz escalation and sticky inflation push the Fed back to hawkish; cash is prudent until the capex picture clarifies tonight.

macro_trigger_map.event: AMZN, GOOG, MSFT report after close — capex and AI revenue guidance in focus
macro_trigger_map.bullish_outcome: All three reaffirm $1.7T capex plan with Azure organic growth >28% and AWS reacceleration — capex thesis validated.
macro_trigger_map.bearish_outcome: Capex guidance cut; Azure AI growth is largely OpenAI circular; AMZN reinvestment fears crystallize.
bearish_impact: NVDA → deeper CAUTION (capex max-out narrative cracks)
GOOG → still CAUTION, but TPU demand thesis holds

macro_trigger_map.event: FOMC rate decision and dot-plot — hawkish hold likely given oil, but language on inflation matters
macro_trigger_map.bullish_outcome: Powell signals that oil is transitory; rate-cut window opens for late 2026 — relief for duration-sensitive tech.
bullish_upgrades: BigTech cluster → removal of hard block on capex-heavy names
macro_trigger_map.bearish_outcome: FOMC explicitly cites oil as reason to delay cuts further; dot-plot shifts hawkish — US10Y breaks 4.5%.
bearish_impact: All CAUTION signals remain firmly blocked; BigTech AVOID risks intensify if capex also disappoints.

macro_trigger_map.event: April CPI — first post-tariff print that can validate sticky inflation from oil pass-through
macro_trigger_map.bullish_outcome: CPI comes in cool, showing oil hasn't yet filtered into core — inflation panic recedes.
bullish_upgrades: Singapore banks upgrade potential if US10Y falls toward 4.20%
Semis relax extension blocks as rate fears ease
macro_trigger_map.bearish_outcome: CPI shock, core inflation accelerates — validates stagflation fears and shuts the door on rate cuts entirely.
bearish_impact: All extended semis → AVOID potential if capex and rate both break
Singapore banks → NIM compression deepens; CAUTION across cluster
